title: Paper clay moon mobile
subtitle: Crescent moons and full circles cut from paper clay and hung at different heights
excerpt: A set of crescent moon and circle shapes cut from paper clay, textured and pierced, then hung at different heights on a branch or wooden dowel to make a hanging mobile. Paper clay holds the thin crescent arms without cracking.
sourceNotes: Paper clay slab construction derived from first principles.

content.text: A moon mobile is five to seven clay shapes hung at different heights from a horizontal branch or dowel.

is the better material here because crescent shapes have very thin tips; the cellulose fibres bridge these narrow sections where plain air-dry clay usually cracks. Cut three crescent moons and two or three full circles, texture them differently, and hang them at staggered heights.

items.name: Crescent cutter or card templates
items.substitution: Cut from card: a large circle minus a smaller offset circle

items.name: A branch or wooden dowel, about 40 cm
Waxed cotton cord or fishing line

content.text: 1. Roll a slab and add texture
Roll the paper clay to 5 mm thickness. Press stamps or lace into the surface while soft. Let rest for 5 minutes.

Cut three crescent moons in different sizes (small: 6 cm, medium: 8 cm, large: 10 cm). Cut two or three full circles (6-8 cm diameter). Crescents can be cut by pressing a large round cutter down, then pressing a smaller cutter into the clay at an offset position and removing the inner circle; the remaining crescent shape lifts away.
Pierce a hole 4 mm from the top of each shape with the needle tool. For crescents, place the hole in the thickest part of the crescent body, not in a tip. Make the hole large enough for the cord.
Dry flat on a board under light plastic cover for the first 6 hours, then open. Full drying to

takes one to two days. Seal with PVA or paint and seal. Thread cord through each hole and tie each shape at a different height from the branch, varying the lengths from 5 cm to 30 cm for a layered effect.

items.fix: Use paper clay; cover tips with a narrow strip of plastic while drying
items.cause: Plain air-dry clay used, or dried too fast

items.fix: Test the balance before fixing cord lengths; move shapes along the branch until balanced
items.cause: Shapes distributed unevenly on the branch
